name: ssot-tb
description: SSOT->TB: parse canonical YAML and RTL, derive tests generically, run simulation, emit coverage/VCD, or escalate precise failures

tasks.content: [SSOT->TB] Parse SSOT, RTL, and build verification ledger
tasks.detail: Read the canonical SSOT at <ip>/yaml/<ip>.ssot.yaml or exact path from [SSOT HANDOFF]. Read <ip>/verify/equivalence_goals.json when present; if it is missing, run or request /ssot-equiv-goals <ip> before TB signoff. Read <ip>/list/<ip>.f and all RTL it references. Build a verification ledger: top module, simulator, clock/reset, DUT ports, protocol drivers needed, scenario list, equivalence goal IDs, expected results, FunctionalModel/model-adapter source, scoreboard model, coverage bins, waveform requirements, and known assumptions. SSOT expected behavior is authoritative; never rewrite expected values to match observed RTL. Derive from actual SSOT content; do not assume DMA/APB/register-only scenario names.
tasks.criteria:
SSOT file was read
equivalence_goals.json was read or a precise /ssot-equiv-goals blocker was emitted
RTL filelist and all referenced RTL files were read
Every DUT top port is mapped to a TB/cocotb signal
Every test_requirements.scenarios[] entry has stimulus, SSOT-derived expected result, checker plan, and coverage bin
Every non-blocked equivalence goal has a planned scoreboard event row keyed by goal_id
Coverage bins are planned for reset, each feature, each FSM path, error behavior, and protocol handshakes where present
No fixed IP-specific generator/template was added

tasks.content: [SSOT->TB] Choose verification implementation and write TB artifacts
tasks.detail: Choose cocotb/Python unless the SSOT/project explicitly requires SV-only or cocotb is unavailable. For cocotb: write <ip>/tb/cocotb/test_<ip>.py and test_runner.py with clock/reset helpers, protocol driver helpers, workflow/tb-gen/runtime/equivalence_scoreboard.py (`EquivalenceScoreboard`) or copied FunctionalModel adapter, scoreboard model, scenario tests, coverage writer, VCD dumping, and <ip>/sim/scoreboard_events.jsonl emission. Any helper that logs [FAIL] or increments failed-check counters must raise AssertionError so cocotb records a real failure. For SV-only: write <ip>/tb/tb_<ip>.sv and scenario task file(s) plus a reference_model/model_adapter path that preserves SSOT/FunctionalModel expected behavior and writes the same scoreboard_events.jsonl fields. All tests must be derived from SSOT scenarios and equivalence goals, not from fixed scenario names.
tasks.criteria:
TB files exist on disk in the chosen layout
python3 workflow/tb-gen/scripts/check_tb_python_compile.py <ip> --root . passes before simulation
python3 workflow/tb-gen/runtime/equivalence_scoreboard.py <ip> --root . --self-check passes when FunctionalModel is present
Clock and reset behavior matches SSOT
DUT instantiation/toplevel matches RTL top module
Every SSOT scenario is represented by a test/subtest
Every non-blocked equivalence goal is checked or has a precise human/SSOT blocker
scoreboard_events.jsonl rows include goal_id, scenario_id, cycle, stimulus, fl_expected, rtl_observed, passed, mismatch, and coverage_refs
Every test has an assertion/checker with expected/got failure text
Any [FAIL] path raises/asserts and cannot be hidden by a PASS summary
Coverage JSON writer or SV report path is implemented
VCD dump path for sim_debug is implemented

tasks.content: [SSOT->TB] Run compile/simulation and repair TB-only issues
tasks.detail: Run the real simulator command after writing TB. Cocotb path: python3 -m pytest -q <ip>/tb/cocotb/test_runner.py --tb=short. SV path: iverilog -g2012 -f <ip>/list/<ip>.f -o <ip>/sim/<ip>.out && vvp <ip>/sim/<ip>.out. If compile fails due to TB syntax, port mapping, runner path, or scoreboard bug, fix TB and rerun. If a scenario fails against the SSOT expected value, do not weaken the checker or change the expected value to observed RTL behavior; stop TB edits and emit [SIM ESCALATE] -> rtl-gen with exact test, expected, got, and suspected RTL file/signal.
tasks.criteria:
Simulation command was run after the last TB edit
Compile/elaboration exits 0
All SSOT scenario tests pass OR failures are escalated precisely
A cocotb PASS summary is rejected if any [FAIL] line or failed SSOT check remains
All non-blocked equivalence goals have scoreboard_events.jsonl evidence before DONE
TB does not modify RTL files
No failure markers remain on DONE path
Results are captured to results.xml or sim_report.txt

tasks.detail: Ensure sim_debug can inspect results. Required outputs on PASS path: <ip>/sim/<ip>.vcd, <ip>/sim/scoreboard_events.jsonl, <ip>/cov/coverage_functional.json when functional bins are tracked, <ip>/cov/toggle.json when VCD parsing is available, <ip>/sim/coverage_report.md, and <ip>/tb/cocotb/results.xml or <ip>/sim/sim_report.txt. Final <ip>/cov/coverage.json is produced by the common coverage stage from SSOT/fcov_plan plus raw evidence. Do not claim instrumented code coverage unless the tool actually produced it.
tasks.criteria:
VCD exists and is non-empty
scoreboard_events.jsonl exists and references equivalence goal IDs
coverage_functional.json exists when functional bins are tracked
coverage report distinguishes raw functional bins from final SSOT coverage signoff
Results XML or sim_report exists
Artifacts are under <ip>/sim, <ip>/cov, or <ip>/tb only

tasks.content: [SSOT->TB] Emit tb-gen result or escalation handoff
tasks.detail: If PASS, output [SSOT RESULT] tb-gen DONE with module, SSOT path, TB files, simulation command, pass count, coverage path, VCD path, and next workflow sim_debug. If failures remain, output one [SIM ESCALATE] -> rtl-gen block per failing behavior with exact expected/got evidence and do not claim DONE.
tasks.criteria:
DONE path includes fresh simulation evidence
DONE path names exact VCD and coverage files
Failure path includes precise SIM ESCALATE blocks
No RTL success beyond simulation evidence is overstated
Next workflow is sim_debug
